title: Right-size memory and timeout
description: Use load measurements to pick memory (which scales CPU) and a timeout above worst-case downstream latency, not defaults.
body: Steps: 1. Profile the function across memory settings; on Lambda CPU scales with memory, so more memory can be faster and cheaper per invocation. 2. Pick the memory that minimizes duration*price for the workload. 3. Measure worst-case downstream call latency under load. 4. Set timeout above that with margin, but not so high that a hung call burns budget. 5. Re-test under peak load. Rollback point: before changing production config. Validation: functions finish before timeout under peak and the chosen memory minimizes cost-latency; a timeout shorter than a real downstream call causes truncated work and retries.
